_scenario.title: Future certification workflow — record identity separate from performance rating
_scenario.note: Illustrates the future registry-resolution model (SECTIONHUB_STRATEGIC_ARCHITECTURE.md §10). No resolution service, certification program, or these records exist yet; all identifiers are illustrative. The attestation boundary shown here is the design's central commitment.

evidence_pointers.note: Pointers only. Report contents and any capacity values live with the issuing laboratory and the engineer of record — not in this record.

attestation_boundary.what_this_record_attests: Record existence and traceability only: this identifier resolves to a registered, deterministic configuration description with the provenance grade shown.
what_this_record_does_NOT_attest: Load capacity or any performance rating
Safety, code compliance, or fitness for purpose
Physical correspondence of the labeled article to this record (no inspection performed or implied)
Conformance with any design or testing standard — that remains with testing laboratories, engineers of record, and the industry's conformance programs
attestation_boundary.principle: A registered deck is an IDENTIFIED deck — nothing more. Identity claims and performance claims are deliberately kept in separate layers so each can be trusted.

refusals.message: Capacity values are refused as configuration fields; ratings are evidence-layer outputs bound to the cited test records.
No GSID exists at deck level; geometry identity applies only to member cross-sections referenced by components.

explanations: The buyer's two questions are answered by two different layers: 'what is this?' by this record; 'what can it hold?' by the cited evidence, read with the engineer of record.
Because the identity half refuses to carry the performance half, it can be automated and trusted.
